index.description: Run a recurring rhythm reliably. Output is the same kind of artefact week after week. Daily standups, weekly metrics reviews, sprint planning, quarterly OKR resets, monthly retros, on-call handoffs.

playbook.summary: Affiliate dashboard noise → monthly clean payouts and a message to top referrers.
"Monthly 1-hour ritual: pull affiliate and referral stats, approve commissions, pay out, message top referrers."
Each sprint produces a committable plan the team actually finishes, based on observed capacity, not nominal.
Q4 to books closed, taxes filed, next-year targets locked, service portfolio refreshed, compliance risks logged.
Run an annual planning cycle that closes the books, files taxes, sets next-year revenue / margin / hiring targets, locks the service portfolio, and surfaces compliance risks before they bite.
"A reset of product strategy: market view, competitor landscape, portfolio bets, multi-year roadmap, and updated north-star. Published artefact replaces the prior strategy memo."

Treat the architecture itself as a versioned artifact. Done = the architecture repo is the single source of truth for ADRs, C4 models, fitness functions, and runbooks, kept current via monthly cade...
Done = the codified fitness functions (perf budget, layering rules, dependency direction, error-budget burn) are run; any newly red signal has an owner and a fix-or-relax decision logged.
"Sustainable ceremony rhythm when PM, dev, and client overlap <2h/day: standups, planning, demos, retros, status all running async-first."
Open backlog of mixed maturity → top-of-backlog spec-ready (AC + complexity tag) each week in 1 hour.

"1-hour weekly session: PM brings business priorities, tech lead brings tech-debt + capacity reality. Output: top-of-backlog is spec-ready (acceptance criteria + estimated complexity), nothing else...."
Backlog under 100 items, every item has an owner plus score plus tag, stale items archived; the top-20 ready-to-pull queue exists.
"For each critical data store: latest backup restored to an isolated environment, integrity-checked, and the restore proof logged."
Every fortnight to one quotable result captured (case study or 1-2 sentence testimonial) with permission, in reusable format.
Opinion-only retro → data-driven 45-min retro that exits with 2-3 owned experiments.

"Team runs a 45-min retro at sprint close. Inputs are not opinions only — also: AI-aggregated mistake-memory entries since last retro, alert volume, on-call load, PR-cycle time. Outputs are 2-3 conc..."
One weekly source → fanout to X, IH, newsletter on a sustainable solo cadence.
For each capacity-bound resource (compute, db connections, queue depth, storage), current utilization vs.
End-of-sprint demo to client stakeholders (often non-technical) → explicit acceptance per AC, action items captured, senior dev runs it solo (not the PM).
"End-of-sprint demo to client stakeholders (often non-technical, often executive). Senior dev runs it solo or as tech lead, not the PM. Outcome: explicit acceptance per AC, captured action items."
"Across IaC-managed resources, `terraform plan` (or equivalent) returns clean or every drift item has a documented reason + remediation path."
All cron and scheduled jobs (k8s CronJob, GHA scheduled, Airflow / cloud scheduler) are inventoried; orphan / failing / silently-skipping jobs are flagged and assigned owners.

"Done = the architects / leads of dependent teams have aligned on this week's interface changes, queue contract changes, capacity needs, and shared infra moves; risks are owned; no surprise integrat..."
"Post a written standup that survives 6-9h offset → blockers surfaced inside client SLA, no 'why didn't you say something' escalations."
"Collect last week's thumbs-down / support tickets / NPS comments that touch the AI feature; cluster, route to engineering or product, feed worst into eval set."
All inbound feature requests and complaints from the previous week routed into the backlog with severity, theme tag, and a verdict (now / later / never); the requester is acknowledged.
Unread customer messages → zero unread + top 3 themes promoted to product feedback.
"Zero unread customer messages: each ticket resolved or scheduled, top 3 themes pushed into product feedback, churn signals flagged, FAQ updated where the same answer was given twice."
"Every morning the marketer knows: what posts went out, what bombed, what's queued, what needs a fire-fix. No tab-graveyard, one tracked dashboard view, one decision."
"Inbox-zero on the alert console: each fired alert is either acknowledged with a follow-up ticket, silenced with an expiry + reason, or closed as auto-resolved noise."
Empty page → one published build-in-public tweet thread by EOD.
"10-min async ritual to blockers surfaced, day's deliverables confirmed, founder unblocked from being the bottleneck."
"Founder runs a 10-minute async standup with 1-3 contractors. Output: blockers surfaced, day's deliverables confirmed, founder unblocked from being the bottleneck."

Done = each PR touching a public interface, persistence schema, service boundary, or shared infra config gets a written architect verdict (approve / request-changes / escalate-to-ADR) with rational...
"Tomorrow's prompt/version bump might regress → today's run confirms last 24h still passes the golden set."

Eval suite (golden + canary set) ran overnight; engineer reviews dashboards, files an incident if any metric breached threshold, otherwise green-flags the day. Output is a one- line eval verdict in...
"30-min daily ritual: inbox-zero on Figma comments, resolve open threads, escalate blockers, log unresolved decisions, snapshot the file in version history."
Daily flaky-test triage (morning queue). Triage overnight CI flakes; quarantine, root-cause one, log the rest with reproducer hypotheses so the trust delta is positive by standup.
"Every active ad account inspected once a day. Catch runaway spend, dead creatives, broken pixels before they cost real money. Output: pause / scale / leave alone per campaign."
"Inbox-zero on review queue: every assigned PR has either an approve, a concrete change request, or a documented block. Own PRs are rebased, CI-green, and re-requested."
Status-theater standup → 15-min exception-driven sync sitting on top of an AI-generated pre-brief.

"Distributed team walks into the 15-min sync with the AI pre-brief already posted in the channel: yesterday's merges per person, blocked PRs, failing CI jobs, Sentry/Datadog regressions, today's int..."
Show up to delivery standup with current blockers, pending clarifications, and any ACs that need dev sign-off, in under 15 minutes total. Repeats every working day.
All direct deps are at latest stable OR explicitly pinned with a reason. Known CVEs are patched or risk-accepted in writing. Build is green; e2e suite ran on bumped versions.
"Real (not paper) DR exercise: pick scenarios, drill recovery against backups + IaC, measure actual RTO/RPO, fix gaps, publish refreshed plan."
"A 1-hour ceremony at sprint start: confirm the goal, pull capacity-fit work, leave with a committed backlog, owner per item, and a sprint goal everyone can repeat."
Drifting feature flags → weekly tech-lead+PM review with explicit per-flag verdict (dark / ramp / GA / kill).

"Tech lead + PM review every open feature flag weekly. For each: keep dark / ramp / GA / kill. Decision criteria are explicit (SLO impact, error rate, adoption metric). Flags that drift out of lifec..."
"Inbox-zero on bug tracker: Sentry / GitHub issues grouped, severity-tagged, top-3 fixed or queued, debt list updated with one decision per item (fix now / accept / kill)."
Open bug tracker + sprawling debt list → grouped, severity-tagged, top-3 resolved or queued.
30-min Friday ritual to AR follow-up list + utilization heat map + go/no-go on next contractor hire.
"Founder reviews cash position, AR, AP, contractor utilization. Output: AR follow-up list + utilization heat map + go/no-go on next contractor hire."
"30-minute KPI scan: MRR / churn / activation / signup funnel reviewed, anomalies flagged, one experiment chosen for next week, one growth lever pushed."
"Foggy sense of last week's KPIs → 30-minute scan + one experiment chosen for next week."
"End-of-week recap to each active client: shipped / blocked / next, plus hours logged. Done = report sent, invoice draft updated, retainer-cap watch list refreshed."
"Project glossary stays canonical: new terms from this week's tickets and transcripts are defined; conflicting definitions are reconciled in one page."
"A 30-min recurring 1:1 that produces (a) one growth or blocker insight, (b) one decision or commitment from the PM, (c) updated team-development notes. Not a status update — explicitly not."
Weekly intake of design patterns, competitor moves, new component libraries; filed by problem-space; one usable pattern queued for application this sprint.
Monthly invoice run. Done = invoices out by 1st, payment received by 15th, late ones escalated by 30th without burning the relationship.

"Done = mentee leaves the 1:1 with one concrete architectural exercise (read this ADR, refactor this seam, write this design doc) and one piece of feedback tied to a quality attribute or trade-off t..."
"Every newsletter / lifecycle / promo email: pre-flight checked, sent, post-send measured. Deliverability watched, unsubs investigated, winner replicated."

Done = the C4 L1+L2 diagrams in /docs reflect actual deployed topology (services, queues, datastores, external deps) within the last 7 days; drift between diagram and reality is either fixed or fla...

"Monday client check-in cycle (one per active client) — Each active client receives a 5-10 min status note before noon Monday: what shipped last week, what's next, blockers, expected hours. Done = a..."

"Each active client receives a 5-10 min status note before noon Monday: what shipped last week, what's next, blockers, expected hours. Done = all active clients pinged, replies tracked, week's plan..."
Once a week to updated sales pipeline + 3-5 next-step actions assigned per active deal.
"Roadmap re-aligned with last week's signals; 2-3 swimlane bets reconfirmed or flagged; deltas posted to Linear/Productboard; async note shipped to founders + EM by end of day."
"Sharper positioning: top 5 competitors re-audited, pricing & feature deltas captured, 1 positioning tweak shipped, kill-list updated for non-differentiating roadmap items."
Stale competitive picture → sharper positioning + 1 ship + kill-list updates.
One sitting per month to all client invoices sent + all contractors paid + books reconciled.
Random side-projects → a yearly rotation of 4–6 small bets compounding into ramen profitability.

Run a recurring rotation that ships 4–6 small products per year, keeps 2–3 alive based on traction, sunsets the rest, and compounds the surviving ones into a portfolio that reaches ramen profitabil...
PM coordinating 3+ feature teams ships cross-team work without weekly fire drills, surfaces blockers within 24h, no surprise integration breaks.

Run a 3-8 team delivery program (typical Outsource enterprise account or P6 multi-squad product) with stable cross-team dependency flow, predictable inter-team handoffs, and one coherent client/pro...
Outgoing on-call has zeroed the alert inbox, documented open incidents + watch-items, and walked the incoming on-call through the queue.
"One sit-down a week to push outreach forward. 10-20 personalized touches sent, prior week's replies handled, no inbox drift. Outputs queue into the CRM, not the brain."

"A focused block where junior drives, senior navigates. Output: shipped change + at least one captured 'lesson' written into team wiki / AGENTS.md / lint rule so the AI doesn't make the same mistake..."
For each P0/P1 in the past week, a written retro is published with timeline, contributing factors, action items with owners, and learnings filed into the runbook system.
PR review for test coverage of new feature. Review a PR not for code style but for whether the tests actually cover the AC, including the non-happy paths the author skipped.
Routine PR review → paired senior+junior session where AI handles the mechanical floor and human time goes to design + judgment.

Senior + junior pair on a single PR review session. Senior narrates the why behind each comment; AI bot pre-runs the diff to surface obvious lint/security/test-coverage issues so the human time goe...
Done = the top-N production-critical services have current runbooks, on-call rotation, alert routing, and SLO dashboards; any gap is owned with a date; SLO burn-rate trend is reviewed and acted on.
"End of week: open branches are either merged, rebased onto main with no conflicts, or explicitly parked with a reason. Commit history on what merged this week is grep-able."
PH launch noise → every comment and DM answered within 24h, lurkers converted to signups.
Project hits final milestone or retainer ends. Done = handover artifacts delivered, final invoice settled, internal retro logged, referral/upsell motion triggered.
Project hits final milestone or retainer ends. Done = handover artifacts delivered, final invoice settled, internal retro logged, referral/upsell motion triggered.

"Hand over a finished project cleanly: scope verified, contracts closed, handover docs delivered, retrospective captured, benefits-realisation report sent, team released, account warm for next engag..."
Reviewer opens a PR that edits a production prompt template; runs the eval delta, checks regressions, requests changes or approves with rollout plan.
End of quarter to per-account next-quarter plan, 3 upsell targets, churn-risk list with mitigation, and a refreshed reference pool.

"Across the active book of clients, run a structured quarterly business review that surfaces churn risk, upsell, and reference opportunities. Outputs: per-account next-quarter plan, three upsell tar..."
"Quarter starts with a published roadmap, OKRs cascaded from company → product → squad level, prioritised bets locked, capacity reserved, and a written 'what we are NOT doing' anti-roadmap."
Quarter closes with stale OKRs and ad-hoc backlog → next quarter starts with published OKRs, top-3 sized bets, RACI, and a populated backlog.

Quarter closes with retro + lessons learned, next-quarter OKRs published, top 3 feature bets sized + sequenced into roadmap, capacity confirmed per squad, and every role (PM, PdM, Architect, BA, De...

"A recurring (every ~13 weeks) architecture-health review across the team's owned systems. Done = published delta report covering drift from ADRs, debt heatmap, fitness-function results, top 3 risks..."
Disconnected OKRs → cascaded company → team → personal OKRs with weekly check-ins and a quarterly blameless retro.
"Company OKR -> team OKR -> personal OKR with weekly check-ins and a quarterly blameless retro. Output: every dev knows what their work ladders to"
Drifted ICP and stale pricing → adjusted ICP one-liner + 1 queued price experiment.
"Pricing & ICP still match reality: paid-cohort signal reviewed, ICP one-liner adjusted, one price experiment queued or rejected, free-to-paid funnel decision recorded."
"End of every quarter: drop the worst client, raise rates on the rest, fill the freed slot with a higher-rate engagement, and lock the next quarter's revenue target and capacity allocation."
"End of every quarter: drop the worst client, raise rates on the rest, fill the freed slot with a higher-rate engagement, and lock the next quarter's revenue target and capacity allocation."
Every 90 days, decide whether to raise rates, with whom, and by how much. Done = rate decision matrix updated, raise-letters drafted for eligible clients, new prospect quotes adjusted.
Per retainer client every quarter to confirmed renewal, scope adjustment, or planned offboarding.
"A weekly pass over the risk register: re-score top 10 by probability×impact, retire stale risks, add new ones from the week's incidents, set next-step owners."

Continuous flow that runs from project start to closure. Establish, populate, audit, and retire a traceability matrix linking business need → requirement → design → AC → test → release → benefit. E...
"Last week's research artifacts condensed into 3-7 actionable insights with evidence; insights linked to design backlog items; top theme picked for next prototype."

Review and trust AI-generated tests. Establish a review process for tests produced by Claude/Copilot/Cursor — catch happy-path bias, hidden test-implementation coupling, missing edge cases, asserti...

"Establish the minimum communication rhythm for a 2–3 person agency where the founder is also the PM. Replaces standups, retros, and 1:1s with one weekly sync + structured async + a Friday close-the..."
"Founder-as-PM rhythm: one weekly sync + structured async + Friday close - replaces standups/retros/1:1s for 2-3 person teams."
"Weekly grooming: top 20 backlog items are readable, sized, dependency-mapped. Exit: 2 sprints of well-formed work ready to pull."

"A tight 15-minute ceremony where each contributor names yesterday's done, today's plan, and a real blocker; the PM exits with a refreshed blocker list and at most one parking-lot follow-up scheduled."
"A weekly call across teams (P4 across vendors / client side; P6 across squads): align on handoffs, surface late commitments, and lock the next-week handoff schedule."
"Weekly 30-minute pass: confirm no one is >100% allocated, find idle capacity, rebalance across projects or squads."

"Two ceremonies at sprint close: review (showcase plus stakeholder feedback) and retro (team-internal improvement). Exit with a reviewed increment plus 1-3 concrete action items owned by named people."
"A single secret (DB password, API key, service-account credential) is rotated with zero or planned downtime: new value provisioned, consumers cut over, old value revoked, audit trail complete."
Security scan triage (weekly). Walk SAST/DAST/dep-scan output once a week; convert noise into a small, ranked, owner-tagged worklist.

Senior promotion case prep (every 6 months) — Half-day reflective task done before promotion review cycle. Produces a written case that maps your last 6 months at the outsource shop to the senior/l...
"For each tier-1 service: current SLO compliance and remaining error budget known; if burn rate exceeds policy, release freeze or focus shift recommended."
Chaotic week with context-switch tax → honest weekly cadence with theme-days and focus discipline.
"An honest operating rhythm for a one-person SaaS: which day for shipping, which for marketing, which for inbox, plus the kill-switch for context-switching that destroys solo productivity."
Sprint top-N ranked, each item is acceptance-criteria + story-pointed-ready, dependencies surfaced; the planning meeting runs in 45 minutes instead of 2 hours.

"Spec-ready stories from grooming become SDD feature folders: spec.md + design.md (where needed) + implementation-plan.md broken into TASK_*.md files. Capacity decided from team velocity and complex..."
"60-90 min poker session for the next sprint → offshore team scale calibrated with client scale, preventing 'why did your 3 take a week' debates."
"Run or participate in a 60-90 min poker session for the next sprint. Calibrate offshore team's scale with client team's scale, prevent 'why did your 3 take a week' debates."
Friday-clear backlog → Monday-morning task primed with prompt and acceptance.
"Friday-clear backlog: top 3 outcomes for the week chosen, single-tasked file/board updated, no-go items deferred, Monday-morning first task primed with prompt + acceptance."
Test-suite health dashboard review. Short standing read of suite-level health metrics; spot trend reversals before they hit release.
Catch cost spikes within 24h → decide if model-routing, caching, or context-engineering change is needed before billing close.
Once a week the matrix reflects every shipped story, every open AC, and every stranded requirement. Stranded items get an owner and a date by end of session.
Travel-day reality → keep momentum without coding, via content, comms, and planning only.
"A daily ~15-minute ritual: refresh the chart, read the slope, choose exactly one corrective lever (re-scope, re-assign, raise risk, or do nothing on purpose)."
Monthly SaaS audit to kill list + consolidation plan + projected savings.
All container images and dependency manifests scanned within the past 7 days; critical/high CVEs either patched or have an exception with expiry; SBOM diff posted to security channel.
"Every Friday: any test that has reached significance gets a verdict. Losers killed, winners promoted, undecided extended or stopped. No 'eternal experiments'."
Risk of architectural drift → 45-min weekly review with explicit pattern-promotion + ADR queue + design-doc updates.

"Architect + 2-3 senior engineers walk the week's significant PRs + open RFCs. Goal: spot drift early (cross-service coupling, layering violations, missed reuse), promote good patterns to team patte..."
Weekly bug-bash facilitation (Friday). Run a 90–120 minute cross-role bash on a single risk area; convert hour-of-attention into a ranked defect list.
One sitting to 3-8 client status emails sent, each anchored to scope + next milestone, scope-creep flagged.
"End-of-week RAG report that the offshore senior owns: progress vs sprint goal, risks, asks. Client PM forwards it upstream without rewriting. Pre-empts Monday-morning interrogation."
End-of-week RAG report that the offshore senior owns → client PM forwards upstream without rewriting, pre-empts Monday interrogation.
Last 7 days of cloud spend reviewed against budget by service / team; top three anomalies have owners + actions; rightsizing candidates queued.
"Every Wednesday: top 3 competitors' new content, ads, pricing, SERP gainers in our cluster. Output is 0-3 'react now' items + a slowly-growing intel doc."
Empty content slot → 1 indexed asset live each week (blog / changelog / doc).
"Cross-functional review of in-flight designs: decisions captured, action items assigned with owners, design-system impacts surfaced."
"Engineer reviews last week's spend by feature / model / prompt; identifies top-3 cost levers; ships one cost cut OR documents why current spend is optimal."
Empty week → one newsletter issue scheduled in Substack or Beehiiv by Friday.
"Multi-product noise → one product that earns the next week's attention."
North-star plus 3-5 leading indicators reviewed against target band; deviations have a hypothesis; one action item is filed per red metric.
"Last week's prompt-version candidates → one merged, one rolled-back, one parked."
"Engineer samples N production queries from last week, labels retrieval quality (hit@k, MRR, faithfulness), files tickets for the worst clusters."
"Every Monday: know which queries gained/lost impressions, which pages dropped, which content cluster is winning. Decide 1-3 actions for the week (refresh / internal-link / promote / kill)."

"Done = the team's tech-debt board has each item tagged with (1) impact on a quality attribute, (2) cost-of-delay, (3) effort tier; the top 3 items have an owner and an entry in the next sprint cand..."
Last 7 days of X data → next-week content slate rooted in evidence.
"A single 1-page report: RAG status, delivered/planned, top 3 risks, decisions needed, EVM headline (if pro tier). Generated once a week, scannable in 60 seconds by the reader."
"Across 4-6 weeks at year-end: close books, file taxes / VAT correctly across jurisdictions, refresh contracts + insurance, and publish a yearly retrospective that doubles as a marketing asset."
"Across 4-6 weeks at year-end: close books, file taxes / VAT correctly across jurisdictions, refresh contracts + insurance, and publish a yearly retrospective that doubles as a marketing asset."
